function.name: list_foods
function.description: List the user's food catalog. Each food has an "id", a "name", and per-100g macros (calories_100, protein_100, carbohydrates_100, fat_100). Use the returned "id" as the food_id when building a recipe with create_recipe.

function.name: create_foods
function.description: Add one or more foods to the user's catalog in a single transaction and get them back (each including its new "id", in the same order). Always pass an array, even for a single food. Use this when foods a recipe needs are not already in the catalog, then reference the returned ids as food_id values in create_recipe. All macro values are per 100 grams of the food.

foods.description: The foods to add (at least one). Each item is a JSON object {"name": string, "calories_100": number, "protein_100": number, "carbohydrates_100": number, "fat_100": number}. All macro values are per 100 grams of the food.

function.name: create_meal
function.description: Log a meal the user ate. Records the meal and its foods; the totals feed the nutrition summary.

foods.description: The foods in the meal (at least one). Each item is a JSON object {"name": string, "weight_g": number > 0, "calories": number, "protein": number, "carbohydrates": number, "fat": number}. The macro values are TOTALS for the given weight_g, not per-100g.

function.name: list_meals
function.description: List the user's logged meals, each with its foods and macro totals. Omit the date fields to get all meals, or pass a date range to filter.

date_from.description: Optional start date, YYYY-MM-DD. Omit or pass "" to list all meals.

date_to.description: Optional end date, YYYY-MM-DD. Omit or pass "" to list all meals.

function.name: log_workout
function.description: Log a workout the user performed. Records the exercises with weight and reps; the totals feed the training summary.

routine_id.description: Optional. Pass "" for an ad-hoc workout, or an id from list_routines to log a saved routine.

exercises.description: The exercises performed (at least one). Each item is a JSON object {"name": string, "weight_kg": number (0 or more), "reps": integer (1 or more)}.

function.name: list_workouts
function.description: List the workouts the user actually performed, each with its exercises (weight and reps). Omit the date fields for today's workouts, or pass a date range (e.g. for "this week").

date_from.description: Optional start date, YYYY-MM-DD. Omit or pass "" for today.

date_to.description: Optional end date, YYYY-MM-DD. Omit or pass "" for today.

function.name: list_routines
function.description: List the user's saved workout routines (reusable templates), each with its id, name, and entries. Pass a routine's id as routine_id to log_workout to log that saved routine.

function.name: create_routine
function.description: Create a reusable workout routine (a saved training template) for the user. Records the planned exercises with target weight and reps. The saved routine can later be logged with log_workout by passing its id as routine_id. Use this to save a routine for future use; use log_workout to record a workout the user actually performed.

entries.description: The planned exercises (at least one). Each item is a JSON object {"name": string, "weight_kg": number (0 to 1000), "reps": integer (1 to 100)}.

function.name: create_recipe
function.description: Create a recipe built from foods already in the catalog. Every ingredient must reference an existing food by its food_id — get these from list_foods, or create the foods first with create_foods. Do not invent food_id values.

notes.description: Optional free-text notes for the recipe.

ingredients.description: The recipe's ingredients (at least one). Each item is a JSON object {"food_id": string (from list_foods/create_foods, never invented), "weight_g": number > 0, "notes": string (optional)}.

function.name: list_recipes
function.description: List the user's recipes, each with its id, name, notes, and ingredients (the foods and their weights). Use this to find a recipe's id by name before calling get_recipe.

function.name: get_recipe
function.description: Get a single recipe by id, with its ingredients (foods) and notes. Find the id with list_recipes first.

id.description: The recipe id, obtained from list_recipes.

function.name: get_summary
function.description: Get a combined nutrition + training summary (calories, macros, sets, reps, volume) over an inclusive YYYY-MM-DD date range.

date_from.description: Start date, YYYY-MM-DD. Empty ("") defaults to today.

date_to.description: End date, YYYY-MM-DD. Empty ("") defaults to today.
